results.rendered_html:
dāsī f.
1〉 Sclavin s. u. 1. dāsa 2.
— 2〉 adj. dämonisch s. u. 2. dāsa.
— 3〉 N. einer Pflanze, = nīlā jhiṇṭī Barleria caerulea  AK. 2,4,2,55 .  MED. s. 3 . = pītā jhiṇṭī  RATNAM. _im_ ŚKDR.  = jhiṇṭī  TRIK. 3,3,446 .  H. an. 2,583 . = kākajaṅghā und nīlāmlāna  RĀJAN. _im_ ŚKDR. 
— 4〉 N. pr. eines Flusses  MBH. 6,338 .  VP. 184 .
— 5〉 = vedī  VIŚVA _im_ ŚKDR. 
— 6〉 Hure  WILS.

results.rendered_html:
dāsī
1 A female servant or slave.
2 The wife of a fisherman.
3 The wife of a Śūdra.
4 An altar.
5 A harlot.
Comp.
— putraḥ,
— sutaḥ the son of a female slave.
— śrotriyaḥ a Brāhmaṇa (knowing the Vedas) attached to a female slave.
— sabhaṃ a collection of female slaves. (The gen. sing. dāsyāḥ enters into some compounds, but loses its literal sense; e. g. dāsyāḥputraḥ,
— sutaḥ ‘a whore-son’, used as a term of abuse; dāsyāḥputraiḥ śakunilubdhakaiḥ  Ś. 2 ; but dāsyāḥ sadṛśī ‘like
(pb) a female slave’).
